sample.title: U-571
sample.plot: In the midst of World War II, the battle below the seas rages. The Nazis have the upper edge as the Allies are unable to crack their war codes. That is, until a wrecked U-boat sends out an SOS signal, and the Allies realize this is their chance to seize the 'enigma coding machine'. But masquerading as Nazis and taking over the U-boat is the smallest of their problems. The action really begins when they get stranded on the U-boat.

sample.title: Where Eagles Dare
sample.plot: During World War II, a British aircraft is shot down and crashes in Nazi held territory. The Germans capture the only survivor, American Brigadier General George Carnaby (Robert Beatty), and take him to the nearest S.S. headquarters. Unknown to the Germans, the General has full knowledge of the D-Day operation. The British decide that the General must not be allowed to divulge any details of the Normandy landing at all costs, and order Major Jonathan Smith (Richard Burton) to lead a crack commando team to rescue him. Amongst the team is an American Ranger, Lieutenant Morris Schaffer (Clint Eastwood), who is puzzled by his inclusion in an all British operation. When one of the team dies after the parachute drop, Schaffer suspects that Smith's mission has a much more secret objective.

sample.title: Último Recurso
sample.plot: A mysterious package arrives at the office of a once prestigious sports magazine suggesting that the first Football World Cup took place in 1926 but it was erased from history.

sample.title: War Pigs
sample.plot: Disgraced World War 2 Army Captain Jack Wosick is given the opportunity for redemption when asked to lead a rag-tag unit of misfits known as the War Pigs on a secretmission to go behind enemy lines to uncover and capture a Nazi developed Super Weapon the V3, a massive artillery canon that would give the Nazis an insurmountable advantage against the Allies. With the help of Captain Hans Picault, a German Anti-Nazi serving with the French Foreign Legion and Colonel AJ Redding, a battle hardened WW1 veteran, Jack must train, lead and earn the respect of his new squad to become a functioning reconnaissance unit.

sample.title: Invitation to a Murder
sample.plot: 1934. Miranda Green and five other strangers, are invited to the remote island mansion of billionaire Lewis Findley. As the weekend progresses the clues about why they have been invited begin to unfold along with a sinister mystery.

sample.title: Righteous Thieves
sample.plot: The head of a secret organization assembles a crew to steal back artwork plundered by Nazis during World War II.

sample.title: City Lights
sample.plot: A tramp falls in love with a beautiful blind girl. Her family is in financial trouble. The tramp's on-and-off friendship with a wealthy man allows him to be the girl's benefactor and suitor.

sample.title: Greyhound
sample.plot: Based upon the novel "The Good Shepherd" by C S Forester, this is the thrilling story of an Allied convoy crossing the North Atlantic in 1942 as it faces relentless attack by a German submarine wolf pack. The leader of the convoy's destroyer screen is a US Navy commander making his first Atlantic crossing. The story focuses on his command responsibility as he fights the cold, the relentless night, the brutal sea, and his deep fatigue as he chases down the attacking submarines in the deadly game of cat and mouse. The exciting story, a thrilling ride-along with the beleaguered captain, so deeply portrays the elements of battle command that for a long period of time the book was used as a text at the US Naval Academy.

sample.title: Rio the Survivor
sample.plot: Rio has been sick since birth, lives without parents, and is raised only by his grandmother. Rio is a smart boy and good at playing football. His dream is to become a national team player. But he is experiencing a lot of discrimin...

sample.title: Murder on the Orient Express
sample.plot: Hercule Poirot, the best detective in the world, decides to travel on the Orient Express. The train accidentally gets stopped because of a small avalanche. Little did he know that a murder was planned and that a person on this train was capable of committing such crime.

sample.title: Atlantis: The Lost Empire
sample.plot: 1914: Milo Thatch, grandson of the great Thaddeus Thatch works in the boiler room of a museum. He knows that Atlantis was real, and he can get there if he has the mysterious Shephards journal, which can guide him to Atlantis. But he needs someone to fund a voyage. His employer thinks he's dotty, and refuses to fund any crazy idea. He returns home to his apartment and finds a woman there. She takes him to Preston B. Whitmore, an old friend of his Grandfathers. He gives him the shepherds journal, a submarine and a 5 star crew. They travel through the Atlantic ocean, face a large lobster called the Leviathan, and finally get to Atlantis. But does the Atlantis crew have a lust for discovery, or something else?

sample.title: Re/Member
sample.plot: A high school student and her friends are trapped in a time loop by a ghost and the only way to escape is to find the corpse of the ghost's previous victim.

sample.title: The Outlaws
sample.plot: Set in the 1920s, two drifters are being chased by the police after a failed train robbery.

sample.title: The Legend of 1900
sample.plot: 1900. Danny Boodmann, a stoker on an American passenger liner, Virginian, finds a baby abandoned on the ship. He names the child Danny Boodmann T.D. Lemon Nineteen Hundred '1900' and raises the child as his own until his death in an accident on the ship. The child never leaves the ship and turns out to be a musical genius, especially when it comes to playing the piano. As an adult he befriends a trumpet player in the ship's band, Max Tooney. After several years on the ship Max leaves, and tells the story of 1900 to the owner of a music store.

sample.title: The Wrath of God
sample.plot: Luciana, a young woman immersed in a circle of mysterious deaths of relatives and the growing suspicion that the person responsible is an enigmatic writer, her former boss.

categories.label: oceans turned into contested war roads
categories.description: In this age, the sea is not open distance but a vital passage where food, fuel, troops, and messages must run a gauntlet of attack. What you'll see: convoys in rough water, destroyers screening merchant ships, sonar pings, depth charges, and the long cold watch on dark decks.

categories.label: cipher machines guarding military secrets
categories.description: Victory depends not only on guns but on hidden signals, codebooks, and the devices that turn orders into unreadable traffic. What you'll see: radio rooms, intercepted transmissions, guarded documents, hurried decoding work, and desperate efforts to seize or protect enemy cipher gear.

categories.label: occupied lands under German control
categories.description: Much of Europe lives under foreign rule, with checkpoints, garrisons, intelligence offices, and fear built into ordinary movement. What you'll see: uniforms in town streets, mountain strongholds, border inspections, collaborators and resisters, and travel conducted under false papers or suspicion.

categories.label: special units sent behind enemy lines
categories.description: Regular armies are supplemented by small picked bands trained for sabotage, reconnaissance, and raids far from the front. What you'll see: parachute drops, secret briefings, disguised soldiers, demolition work, and missions aimed at bridges, guns, headquarters, or captured personnel.

categories.label: weapons races feeding wartime urgency
categories.description: Every belligerent hunts for a device, gun, vessel, or method that might break the deadlock and alter the balance of the war. What you'll see: experimental artillery, advanced submarines, guarded workshops, technical chatter among officers, and fierce interest in enemy inventions.

categories.label: railways and liners tying continents together
categories.description: Even in wartime and its shadow, long-distance travel still depends on grand trains and steamships that carry strangers, class divisions, and news across great distances. What you'll see: sleeping cars, dining saloons, boiler rooms, port departures, station platforms, and the etiquette of enclosed travel.

categories.label: class lines visible in everyday dress
categories.description: Society remains sharply graded by wealth, education, accent, and birth, and those divisions shape who commands, who serves, and who moves freely. What you'll see: officers and enlisted men kept apart, mansions and cramped rooms, formal evening clothes, servants at hand, and deference expected in speech and bearing.

categories.label: art and treasures uprooted by conquest
categories.description: War does not spare culture, and paintings, relics, and collections are seized, hidden, traded, or claimed as spoils by occupying powers. What you'll see: crates in cellars, catalogues and provenance papers, guarded galleries, aristocratic collections, and secret efforts to recover looted works.

categories.label: mass conflict pressing on civilian life
categories.description: The great war reaches beyond battlefields into homes, livelihoods, travel, and the hopes of people trying to keep ordinary routines alive. What you'll see: blackouts, shortages, anxious telegrams, disrupted journeys, damaged cities, and moments of music, sport, or romance carried on under strain.

categories.label: faith in machines and expert skill
categories.description: This world places enormous trust in trained hands and modern apparatus, from engines and radios to gunsights and navigation tables. What you'll see: engine rooms, plotting tables, periscopes, switchboards, technical manuals, and specialists whose knowledge is treated as a weapon in itself.
